prompt:
A small 3D-printed tugboat (Benchy) about 60 mm long sits on a smooth build plate, its layered hull, cabin, and smokestack clearly showing the fine details used to evaluate print quality. The model is printed in a single color of PLA, with subtle layer lines visible along the curved bow and cabin roof, and the tiny portholes and texturing are crisp but not exaggerated. A strand of filament remains lightly curled near the base, hinting at the roughly 5 m of filament consumed.

The scene is a near-future solarpunk maker space with a desktop FDM 3D printer in the background, soft ambient lighting, and neatly organized spools of filament on a shelf. The 512×512 image is photorealistic with gentle stylization, no logos or readable text on the printer or model, and a composition that centers the Benchy on the build plate while the rest of the printer and workspace remain softly out of focus, emphasizing it as a calibration print.
